mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This mechanism operates continuously when rates remain in the zero-lower-bound zone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2015-06-15 indicates sustained zero-rate policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and rising household income expectations. This triggers increased consumer spending and corporate earnings revisions upward, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as market participants reprrice growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, equity investors reduce growth expectations and rotate away from cyclical equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism requires both demand proxies to move together to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 442,032 million and HOUST at 1,036 thousand as of 2015-05-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling slowing manufacturing activity and potential supply-side constraints. This deceleration raises input cost pressures and reduces corporate profit margins, causing equity investors to lower earnings forecasts and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where INDPRO growth falls below 0.5%, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real interest rate regime. This regime signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, reducing the real cost of equity financing and supporting equity valuations. The mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when this real-rate compression is active.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.031 and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2015-05-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances where real rates are negative (CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%), or the interaction coefficient between inflation and policy rate is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% in a single month, signaling an unexpected deterioration in labor market conditions. This triggers a flight-to-safety rotation out of equities and into fixed income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as risk sentiment reverses.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,100 thousand units annualized, signaling strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence. This acceleration indicates robust household formation and wealth effects from housing appreciation, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings. The mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: housing-led expansion or late-cycle demand strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where HOUST exceeds 1,100 thousand units, or Rank IC between HOUST levels and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.0% annualized, signaling a synchronized supply-demand expansion. This joint acceleration indicates the economy is operating near potential with rising capacity utilization, supporting corporate profit margins and equity valuations. The mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions are met.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 1.0% AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0%
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand expansion or mid-cycle acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% and GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the interaction coefficient between industrial production and GDP growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.
